headline: The impossible dream of the universal remote
summary: Universal remotes promise to control all your devices. Many companies failed to perfect the concept. The Harmony remote came closest to fulfilling this "impossible dream."

headline: Solid-state batteries still aren’t ready, but gels are
summary: Solid-state batteries are not yet commercially viable. "Gels" or semi-solid-state batteries offer a promising interim solution. This tech aims to improve on current lithium-ion batteries.

headline: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
summary: White House ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 was reportedly triggered by Amazon. Amazon cybersecurity research and CEO Andy Jassy's talks influenced the decision. The ban involves export control directives impacting advanced AI models.

headline: X-Men ’97 has what Master of the Universe is missing
summary: Marvel's X-Men '97 and Mattel's Masters of the Universe aim to capitalize on childhood nostalgia. X-Men '97's second season will send mutants into an apocalyptic future. The article contrasts the perceived success or approach of X-Men '97 versus Masters of the Universe adaptations.

headline: Rockstar Co-Founder Dan Houser Says There’s No Right Way to Play an Open-World Game
summary: Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser spoke at the 2026 Tribeca Festival. He discussed open-world game design and interconnected multimedia universes. Houser believes players have freedom to experience open-world games uniquely.

headline: Microsoft CEO Says 'There's More Monetization of Xbox Games Happening on YouTube Than at Microsoft'
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella claims YouTube monetizes Xbox content more than Microsoft. This follows Xbox boss Asha Sharma's commitment to "reset" the gaming division. The statement highlights potential missed opportunities for direct monetization within Xbox.

headline: George Miller Reportedly Wants to Make One More Mad Max Movie and a TV Show Before Selling the Franchise
summary: George Miller plans one final Mad Max movie and a TV show. He is reportedly seeking studios to finance these projects. After these, Miller intends to sell the Mad Max franchise rights.

headline: X-Men '97 Season 2, Episodes 1-4 Review (Spoiler-Free)
summary: The first four episodes of X-Men '97 Season 2 maintain high quality. It continues to blend Marvel nostalgia with deep character drama. The review is spoiler-free and highly positive.

headline: Royal Marines board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
summary: Royal Marines and National Crime Agency officers boarded a Russian oil tanker. The operation lasted six hours in the English Channel. The tanker is part of a "shadow fleet."

headline: Watch: MOD video shows Russian shadow fleet tanker interception
summary: British armed forces intercepted a Russian "shadow fleet" oil tanker. The incident occurred in the English Channel. A Ministry of Defence video shows the boarding operation.

headline: Why Haiti v Scotland was antidote to the ills of world football
summary: Scotland secured a nervy but important win against Haiti. The article suggests the match offered a refreshing contrast to broader football issues. Tom English reports from Boston on the victory.

headline: Tech firms had 'enough time' says Nandy, ahead of social media announcement
summary: Lisa Nandy states tech firms have had ample time to address online safety. The Prime Minister is expected to announce new measures on online protection. New regulations will focus on safeguarding teenagers online.
